methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Joby Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

private.name: Archer Aviation
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Public), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
ideaForge is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $50M+ to date. The relevant peer universe consists of six private companies operating in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Nuro, Elroy Air, and Aurora Innovation. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment, limiting traditional public-market benchmarking approaches.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While four same-sector peers exist, the combination of stage filters, adjacent-stage proximity, and valuation-tier matching yielded fewer than the three minimum datapoints required for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given the specialized nature of autonomous systems development, regulatory risk, and capital intensity distinguishing this cohort from broader technology or aerospace sectors.

The absence of sufficient narrow-set comparables and lack of public trading data prevents execution of standard DCF-anchored triangulation at this time. Any forward valuation estimate would require either expansion of the relevant peer set through broader sector inclusion, collection of additional transaction or financing data from the identified private peers, or development of asset-based or revenue-multiple methodologies specific to autonomous systems commercialization timelines. The primary valuation uncertainty reflects the early-stage commercialization phase and heterogeneous business models across the peer set.
